channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: From liberation hero to merchant of division: How Jacob Zuma is squandering the last remnants of his legacy @nehandaradio
item.description: As South Africa heads toward local elections, Gabriel Manyati examines why Jacob Zuma’s association with anti-immigrant politics could permanently reshape how history remembers the former president. The post From liberation hero to merchant of division: How Jacob Zuma is squandering the last remnants of his legacy appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Cross-Border Bus Fares Soar as Zimbabweans Flee Xenophobia in South Africa
item.description: Cross-Border Bus Fares Soar as Zimbabweans Flee Xenophobia in South Africa Thousands of Zimbabweans leaving South Africa amid growing anti-migrant tensions are facing another challenge, with some cross-border transport operators accused of sharply increasing fares and luggage cha #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: DEADLY NEW TYPE OF CANCER: Karoi man loses his PEN!S after sleeping with married women, gets dumped by his wife and 4 children
item.description: A Life Undone: The Agony of Remigio Tom and Zimbabwe’s Battle with Belief and Disease Karoi – The story of Remigio Tom, a 47-year-old man from Karoi, is a harrowing tale that intertwines personal tragedy with deeply entrenched cultural beliefs and the stark realities of a devasta #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Gerald Takwara returns to the Zimbabwe League
item.description: Gerald Takwara returns to Zimbabwean football, rejoining the domestic league as fans eagerly anticipate his impact and leadership this season The post Gerald Takwara returns to the Zimbabwe League appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #Sport #ZimbabweSport #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Politburo meets today @zimsituation @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: Source: Politburo meets today – herald Advocate Jacob Mudenda Joseph Madzimure Zimpapers Politics Hub ZANU PF’s top leadership is set to convene a series of high-level meetings beginning today, with the party expected to review the implementation of key resolutions, assess its organisational state and finalise preparations for this year’s Annual National People’s Conference. The [...] The post Politburo meets today appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: SA crisis: Govt scales up repatriation efforts @zimsituation
item.description: Source: SA crisis: Govt scales up repatriation efforts – herald Some of the buses deployed by the Government to ferry willing Zimbabweans back home from South Africa to their respective destinations in Beitbridge yesterday. – Picture: Edward Zvemisha Thupeyo Muleya and Ivan Zhakata GOVERNMENT has intensified the repatriation of Zimbabweans stranded in South Africa, deploying [...] The post SA crisis: Govt scales up repatriation efforts appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: MIDNIGHT DEATH: Police Commissioner Orders Probe as CID Detective Sergeant Milika Laison DIES Soon After Submitting Resignation @PoliceZimbabwe
item.description: Harare – The Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) has launched a comprehensive investigation into the untimely death of Detective Sergeant Milika Laison, a 43-year-old CID officer, who reportedly perished in a road traffic accident on the Masvingo-Beitbridge Road shortly after tendering his resignation. The incident, which occurred on Friday, 25th June 2026, at approximately 10 PM, [...] The post MIDNIGHT DEATH: Police Commissioner Orders Probe as CID Detective Sergeant Milika Laison DIES Soon After Submitting Resignation first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Silencing Emmerson Mnangagwa Critics In God’s Name? Church Leader’s Remarks Spark Concerns About Censorship

Continue reading "Silencing Emmerson Mnangagwa Critics In God’s Name? Church Leader’s Remarks Spark Concerns About Censorship" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Detective’s Death: Cornered ZRP Spokesperson Blames Social Media For Peddling Falsehoods @PoliceZimbabwe
item.description: By A Correspondent| The Zimbabwe Republic Police is attempting to shut down speculation around the fatal crash that killed Detective... item.title: Zimbabweans repatriated from South Africa face possible re-entry ban
item.description: Cape Times Foreign nationals who voluntarily agreed to be repatriated from Cape Town to the Beitbridge border have expressed dismay after learning they would be declared undesirable and barred from re-entering South Africa for up to five years. Since Sunday, the Department of Home Affairs office in Epping has served as the processing centre for [...] The post Zimbabweans repatriated from South Africa face possible re-entry ban appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe moves to digitise mining claims under new cadastre system
item.description: ZIMBABWE is pressing ahead with plans to establish a transparent digital register of all mining claims across the country as part of efforts to strengthen governance and attract fresh investment into the mining sector. Mines and Mining Development Minister Polite Kambamura on Tuesday briefed Cabinet on progress made towards implementing the Zimbabwe Mining Cadastre Registration [...] The post Zimbabwe moves to digitise mining claims under new cadastre system appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: AMH, ZimInd editor Zaba removed from remand as state withdraws charges
item.description: Zimbabwe Independent editor Faith Zaba and her employer, Alpha Media Holdings (AMH), were removed from remand after the National Prosecuting Authority (NPA) withdrew criminal charges against them before plea, bringing the prosecution to an end. Harare magistrate Aplonia Marutya ordered that Zaba and AMH be removed from remand after the state formally withdrew the charges. [...] The post AMH, ZimInd editor Zaba removed from remand as state withdraws charges appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe complete biggest ever Test win after thrashing Bangladesh in Harare
item.description: SABC Sport Innocent Kaia struck a maiden century before the bowlers starred as Zimbabwe recorded their biggest-ever Test victory after thrashing Bangladesh by an innings and 85 runs in Harare. Zimbabwe took the early ascendancy in the contest when Bangladesh were bowled out for just 140 in the first innings with 20-year-old left-arm seamer Newman [...] The post Zimbabwe complete biggest ever Test win after thrashing Bangladesh in Harare appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Building societies’ lowest-paid workers to earn US$660 after new wage deal
item.description: THE Commercial Workers Union of Zimbabwe (CWUZ) has secured a significant wage increase for employees in the building societies sector following the successful conclusion of a new Collective Bargaining Agreement (CBA). The agreement was reached between CWUZ, representing non-managerial employees, and the National Commercial Employers Association of Zimbabwe. Under the new salary structure, the lowest-paid [...] The post Building societies’ lowest-paid workers to earn US$660 after new wage deal appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo
